improvement_hints: Always apply multi-tenancy filters to every database query, including PATCH/PUT operations, not just GET requests.
Ensure that 'Update' schemas strictly follow the pattern of all fields being Optional to allow partial updates via PATCH.
Implement ownership validation logic in addition to RBAC to ensure users can only modify their own records or records within their scope.
Always implement the full scope of endpoints requested, even if it requires modularizing the router into multiple files.
Ensure all Pydantic models intended for ORM mapping include the Config class with from_attributes=True.
For list endpoints, always include basic filtering (e.g., by foreign keys) and sorting to meet production standards.
Always implement the full scope of requested endpoints; partial implementation is a failure in a production context.
Ensure all Update schemas strictly follow the 'all fields optional' pattern to prevent validation errors during partial updates.
Implement sorting and search parameters in list endpoints as standard practice for production HRM systems.
Ensure that the code block content is actually provided in the prompt before requesting a review.
Always verify that multi-tenancy and RBAC are implemented as the first step in any HRM system module.
Always implement multi-tenancy at the database query level using organization_id.
Ensure Create schemas strictly exclude auto-generated fields like UUIDs and timestamps.
Ensure Update schemas have all fields marked as Optional to allow partial updates.
Always include RBAC decorators or dependency checks for every endpoint in an HRM system.
Always ensure the code block is populated before submitting for review.
Adhere to the multi-tenancy requirement by default in all database queries.
Ensure all endpoints are protected by the project's standard RBAC dependency.
Always implement the API endpoints file when providing a module review; schemas alone are insufficient for a functional HRM system.

Always verify that sub-resources (like answers or appraisals) are scoped to the organization_id of the parent record.
